headline: The French intellectuals uniting against the far right, despite their differences
description: The firing of the head of the Grasset publishing house by billionaire Vincent Bolloré has triggered a belated but growing awareness of the scale of the neoconservative ideological push in France's cultural sphere. Despite their differences, intellectuals are beginning to organize to defend their independence.

headline: Pancreatic cancers: New drug doubles survival rate as France rolls out prevention program

Gaza, Iran, Mali: The dangers of the world's rapidly shrinking media access
description: Several global hotspots are locked down by those in power, who, eager to impose their own narrative, keep journalists from witnessing events as they unfold. This censorship is a boon for propagandists.

headline: 'Trump and Netanyahu have turned their own blindness into a weapon of mass destruction'
description: The mere consideration of Mahmoud Ahmadinejad returning to lead Iran speaks volumes about the intellectual collapse of the Israeli-American alliance, observes historian Jean-Pierre Filiu in his column.

headline: Simone Bellotti brings a poetics of restraint to Jil Sanders
description: Noticed for his work at Bally, where he helped reinvigorate the brand, the Italian designer has found at Jil Sander, where he has served as creative director for the past year, a setting to express his taste for minimalism and restrained melancholy.

headline: Sheikha Al-Mayassa Al Thani, the princess spearheading Qatar's artistic ambitions
description: A central figure in the country's cultural policy, the emir's sister has skillfully used the acquisition of prestigious works of art, the hosting of fairs and the creation of museums to build valuable diplomatic ties.

French sociologist, philosopher and intellectual provocateur Edgar Morin has died aged 104
description: Committed to connecting different fields of knowledge to develop his 'complex thought,' this former member of the Resistance and deeply humanist intellectual advocated for a revolution of consciousness. He died in Paris on Friday, his wife confirmed to Le Monde.

headline: The Bolloré protégée spreading Russian propaganda in France
description: Xenia Fedorova, the former director of the now-banned Russian channel RT France, has free rein to advance the Kremlin's version of events without contradiction across the media empire of far-right billionaire Vincent Bolloré.

headline: Why German trains are never on time anymore
description: The German rail network has deteriorated over the past 20 years, to the point that Deutsche Bahn has become a source of national embarrassment.
